name: ChatGPT
description: Conversational AI for writing, coding, and problem-solving
features: Image analysis and DALL-E image generation
pros: Most widely used AI tool globally — huge community and ecosystem
Extremely versatile: writing, coding, research, images, voice
GPTs marketplace with thousands of specialized assistants
Frequent model updates and new feature releases
cons: Quality complaints increased in 2026 — outputs can feel watered down
Ads introduced on free/Go plans (Feb 2026) — controversial move
Expensive at $200/mo for Pro tier
Inconsistent response quality across sessions
recommended_for: Best for developers needing quick coding help and general-purpose AI assistance.

name: Claude
description: AI assistant with extended thinking capabilities
features: 200K token context window for long documents
Artifacts: inline code, SVG, and HTML rendering
Projects with persistent memory across conversations
pros: Exceptional at nuanced writing and long-form content
Best-in-class 200K context window for document analysis
Strong coding performance with Claude 3.5/3.7 Sonnet
Low hallucination rates on factual tasks
cons: Free tier has strict daily usage limits
No built-in image generation
Web search not natively integrated in base product
API costs can add up for high-volume use
recommended_for: Developers needing advanced coding assistance with extensive context analysis.

name: Gemini
description: Google's multimodal AI assistant

plans.name: Gemini Advanced
highlights: Gemini in Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Slides, Meet

features: Native Google Search integration for real-time information
Deep Google Workspace integration (Docs, Gmail, Sheets, Slides)
Multimodal: text, image, audio, video, and code
pros: Unmatched 1M token context window for large documents
Real-time web search built in by default
Best-in-class Google Workspace integration
Strong multimodal capabilities (text, image, audio, video)
cons: Gemini Advanced requires Google One subscription bundled with storage
Can be less precise than Claude on nuanced reasoning
Privacy concerns given Google's data practices
Workspace AI features require separate plan
recommended_for: Developers processing massive codebases needing comprehensive context analysis.

name: Copilot
description: Microsoft's AI assistant powered by GPT-4

plans.name: Copilot Pro
highlights: Copilot in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote

plans.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
highlights: Copilot in Teams, Outlook, Word, Excel

features: Bing web search integration for real-time answers
Microsoft 365 integration (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams)
pros: Free GPT-4 access — most generous free tier among major AI tools
Best Microsoft 365 / Office integration available
Built into Windows 11 — no extra install needed
Real-time web search via Bing included by default
cons: Free tier has session turn limits and slower responses at peak
Microsoft 365 Copilot business plan is expensive at $30/user/mo
Less capable than Claude or ChatGPT for deep reasoning
Heavily tied to Microsoft ecosystem
recommended_for: Best for Microsoft 365 users seeking free GPT-4 access with seamless Office integration.

name: Midjourney
description: AI image generation from text prompts
features: Style reference (--sref) for consistent visual style
pros: Best overall image quality and aesthetic output among AI image generators
Exceptional style consistency with --sref and --cref parameters
Active community and Discord ecosystem for inspiration
Continuous model improvements (V5 → V6 → V6.1)
cons: No free tier — requires paid subscription from $10/mo
No API for programmatic access
Limited fine-grained editing compared to Stable Diffusion
Discord-based workflow can feel unintuitive for new users
recommended_for: Design professionals seeking photorealistic, aesthetically exceptional images with precise style control.

name: DALL-E 3
description: Advanced image generation by OpenAI
features: Precise prompt adherence (major improvement over DALL-E 2)
Integrated directly into ChatGPT for conversational image creation
Inpainting and image editing via API
Natural language prompt refinement through ChatGPT
pros: Best prompt adherence of any image model — follows complex instructions accurately
Seamlessly integrated into ChatGPT for conversational workflows
No separate subscription needed — included in ChatGPT Plus
Clean, developer-friendly API
cons: No free tier for image generation
Less artistic/stylized output compared to Midjourney
Limited editing and inpainting capabilities vs Stable Diffusion
No style reference or character consistency features
recommended_for: Designers needing precise image generation from detailed, complex creative briefs.

name: Stable Diffusion
description: Open-source AI image generation model
features: Open-source model — run locally or in the cloud
ControlNet for precise pose and composition control
LoRA training for custom styles and characters
pros: Completely free when self-hosted — no usage limits
Massive ecosystem of fine-tuned models and LoRAs
Full control: no content restrictions, custom training
ControlNet enables precise composition and pose control
cons: Requires technical setup for local installation
Needs a capable GPU (4GB+ VRAM) for local use
Output quality varies widely depending on model and settings
Steeper learning curve than cloud tools like Midjourney
recommended_for: Best for creators wanting unlimited image generation without cloud costs.

name: Leonardo AI
description: AI art generator for game assets and creative projects
features: Text-to-image generation with multiple model options
Fine-tuned models for game assets and concept art
AI Canvas for inpainting and outpainting
pros: Generous free tier with 150 daily tokens
Excellent for game asset and concept art generation
Custom model training for consistent style
AI Canvas for powerful inpainting workflows
cons: Free tier lacks commercial license
Token system can be confusing for new users
Output quality varies by model selection
Less photorealistic than Midjourney for photography
recommended_for: Game developers needing consistent character and asset generation with a generous free tier.

name: Sora
description: Text-to-video AI model by OpenAI
features: Remix: modify existing videos with prompts
pros: Most realistic physics and motion understanding of any video AI
Integrated into ChatGPT for easy access
Supports diverse video styles from cinematic to animated
Storyboard mode enables multi-scene storytelling
cons: No free tier — requires ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo) minimum
Video length limited to 20 seconds on Pro, 5 seconds on Plus
Generation can be slow during peak times
Limited editing control compared to traditional video tools
recommended_for: Content creators needing realistic video generation from text descriptions quickly.

name: Runway Gen-3
description: AI video generation and editing platform
features: Motion Brush: control movement in videos
Inpainting: remove or replace objects in video
pros: Best professional-grade AI video generation quality
Comprehensive video editing suite beyond just generation
Motion Brush gives precise control over movement
Used by major studios and professional filmmakers
cons: Credits deplete quickly for high-quality generations
Unlimited plan is expensive at $76/mo
Video length limited to 10 seconds per generation
Generation can be slow during peak times
recommended_for: Filmmakers and studios needing professional-grade cinematic AI video generation.

name: Pika
description: AI video creation from text and images
features: Pikaffects: special effects (explosion, melt, crush, etc.)
pros: Pikaffects enable creative special effects not available elsewhere
Affordable entry point at $8/mo
Good for creative and entertainment content
Lip sync feature for talking avatar videos
cons: Video quality not as cinematic as Runway or Sora
Credits deplete quickly for complex generations
Limited video length per generation
Less suitable for professional/commercial use
recommended_for: Content creators seeking affordable video generation with creative special effects capabilities.

name: HeyGen
description: AI video avatars and text-to-video platform
features: Custom avatar creation from your video
pros: Best AI video translation with voice cloning and lip sync
Custom avatar creation from your own video
Professional quality for corporate training and marketing
40+ language support for global content
cons: Credit system can be confusing (1 credit = 1 minute)
Free tier very limited (1 credit/mo)
Business plan expensive at $72/mo
Avatar quality can look artificial for close-up shots
recommended_for: Content creators needing multilingual videos with realistic avatars and preserved voice.

name: GitHub Copilot
description: AI pair programmer for faster coding

plans.name: Enterprise
highlights: Custom fine-tuned models on your codebase

features: Copilot Chat: conversational coding assistant in IDE
Pull request summaries and code review
Supports VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Visual Studio
pros: Best IDE integration — works natively in VS Code, JetBrains, and more
Context-aware suggestions that understand your entire codebase
Copilot Chat enables conversational debugging and explanation
Supports virtually all programming languages
cons: Paid plan required for unlimited use ($10/mo individual)
Can suggest outdated or insecure code patterns
Privacy concerns about code being sent to GitHub servers
Less capable than Cursor for complex multi-file refactoring
recommended_for: Developers who want real-time code suggestions integrated directly into their IDE.

name: Cursor
description: AI-powered code editor for productivity
features: Cmd+K: inline code generation and editing
Agent mode for autonomous task execution
pros: Best multi-file AI editing — Composer can refactor entire codebases
Codebase indexing gives AI full context of your project
Familiar VS Code interface — zero learning curve for existing users
Agent mode handles complex, multi-step coding tasks autonomously
cons: Pro plan required for serious use ($20/mo)
Can be slow on large codebases during indexing
Agent mode can make unexpected changes — requires careful review
Business plan is expensive at $40/user/mo
recommended_for: Developers building complex projects needing AI-assisted multi-file refactoring.

name: Codeium
description: Free AI code completion and chat
features: Integrates with 40+ IDEs (VS Code, JetBrains, Vim, etc.)
Autocomplete for functions, classes, and boilerplate
pros: Completely free for individual developers — no usage limits
Supports the widest range of IDEs (40+) and languages (70+)
Fast and lightweight — minimal performance impact
No data retention for individual users
cons: Free tier lacks advanced features like codebase indexing
Less capable than Cursor for complex multi-file tasks
Smaller training dataset than GitHub Copilot
Enterprise features require significant investment
recommended_for: Best for developers seeking unlimited free AI code completions across extensive language support.

name: Tabnine
description: AI code assistant for all major IDEs
features: Local model option — code never leaves your machine
Integrates with VS Code, JetBrains, Eclipse, and more
Team learning: learns from your team's codebase
pros: Best privacy — local model option keeps code on your machine
SOC 2 certified — trusted by enterprises with strict compliance needs
Team learning adapts to your organization's coding patterns
Works in air-gapped environments with self-hosted deployment
cons: Less capable than Cursor or Copilot for complex AI tasks
Free tier is quite limited compared to Codeium
Pro plan ($12/mo) is pricier than Codeium (free)
Smaller community and fewer updates than competitors
recommended_for: Enterprise developers prioritizing code privacy and security compliance.

name: ElevenLabs
description: AI voice generation and text-to-speech
features: Voice cloning from a short audio sample
Instant voice cloning (30 seconds of audio)
pros: Most realistic voice quality of any TTS platform
Voice cloning from just 30 seconds of audio
Supports 29+ languages with natural accents
Excellent API for developers building voice apps
cons: Free tier limited to 10,000 characters/mo
Voice cloning raises ethical concerns about misuse
Pro plan is expensive at $99/mo for high volume
Generated audio can occasionally have artifacts
recommended_for: Content creators needing ultra-realistic AI voices for podcasts, audiobooks, and videos.

features: Studio editor for video and slide sync
pros: Built-in studio editor for syncing audio with video and slides
120+ voices across 20+ languages
Easy to use — no audio editing experience needed
Good for e-learning, explainer videos, and presentations
cons: Free tier very limited (10 minutes total)
Voice quality not as realistic as ElevenLabs
Annual billing required for best pricing
Limited voice customization compared to ElevenLabs
recommended_for: Content creators needing professional voiceovers for videos and presentations quickly.

Generous free tier with 50 credits/day

Limited control over specific musical elements

Copyright and licensing of AI music remains legally unclear

features: Real-time web search with AI synthesis
Focus modes: Web, Academic, YouTube, Reddit, News

Workflows automate repetitive content creation tasks
